commands.review_summary:
This PR wraps the Nostr profile relay publish timeout race in a `finally` block that clears the per-relay timer handle.

PR surface: Source +8. Total +8 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main races `pool.publish` with an OpenClaw-owned timeout promise but does not retain or clear that timeout handle when publish succeeds first. I did not run a live Nostr profile publish path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Keep open for contributor-provided real behavior proof and ordinary maintainer validation; there is no safe autonomous repair to queue because the missing evidence must come from an after-fix run.

target.title: fix: clear timeout timer in Nostr profile relay publish loop
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where each Nostr relay publish would leave a dangling timer after `Promise.race` completed.

## Why This Change Was Made

Wrap the per-relay `Promise.race` in `try/finally` that clears the timer handle.

## User Impact

No user-visible change. Prevents a minor timer leak when publishing Nostr profiles.

## Evidence

```
extensions/nostr/src/nostr-profile.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  31 passed (31)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 12.31s
```

Same pattern as `src/node-host/with-timeout.ts:37-45`.
target.author: zhanglei99586
